description_long: This stereo sound system consists of two rectangular speaker cabinets with a rich, dark wood finish and a light-colored front baffle. Each cabinet features three circular speaker drivers of varying sizes, likely for bass, midrange, and treble frequencies, arranged vertically. The front of each speaker is partially covered by a removable black mesh grille, which is shown detached and leaning against the cabinets. The rear and sides of the cabinets display a smooth, polished wood texture, and one side reveals a recessed panel with visible connectors for wiring. The overall design is boxy and robust, with clean lines and a classic, vintage-inspired aesthetic. No visible brand logos or control panels are present on the exterior.
description: A pair of vintage-style wooden stereo speakers with three exposed drivers and removable black mesh grilles. The cabinets feature a polished dark wood finish and visible rear connector panels.
description_view_0: Front view showing both speaker cabinets with exposed drivers and the black mesh grille removed and leaning against one unit.
description_view_1: Rear angled view highlighting the dark wood finish, the absence of visible drivers, and a recessed connector panel on the back.
description_view_2: Direct rear and side view showing the geometric wood pattern, both connector panels, and the mesh grille detached from one speaker.
description_view_3: Side view with the mesh grille partially covering the front, revealing the depth and boxy shape of the cabinet.
attribute_speaker_grille_pattern: The speaker grilles are made of a fine black mesh fabric stretched over a rectangular frame, with no visible geometric or decorative patterns.
